regions.text: When parting, people say 'Good morning!', 'Good afternoon!', 'Good evening!', or 'Good night!' instead of 'Good-by!'. They mean 'May all be well with you!'
'Good-by!' is a short way of saying 'God be with you!'. In the past, this was taught to children as part of their English knowledge.
parting: going away from one another. afternoon: after 12:00. evening: late afternoon. noon: 12:00.
God: Being on Whom all depend. religion: interest in man’s deepest needs.
